text.title: product-discovery Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for product-discovery as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Designing experiments to confirm the idea rather than test it — asking leading questions, selecting friendly users, setting easy success thresholds.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Confirmation bias produces validated assumptions that are actually false. Ask "what would have to be true for this to fail?" and design the experiment to find that.

failure-mode.description: Running only customer interviews without behavioral or quantitative validation.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Interviews capture what users say; analytics and experiments capture what they do. Users often say they will use a feature and do not. Combine methods for confidence.

failure-mode.description: Discovery drags on past 4 weeks without a clear decision.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Endless discovery is avoidance in disguise. After 4 weeks you have enough signal to make a decision: proceed, pivot, or kill. "Let's do one more interview" at week 6 is not discovery — it is delay.

failure-mode.description: Team begins building features during the discovery phase.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Building during discovery means discoveries that invalidate the idea arrive after significant investment. Keep experiments cheap; if you are building, you have already decided to proceed.

failure-mode.description: Learnings from experiments are not documented or shared — they exist only in the PM's head.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Undocumented learnings evaporate. The same assumptions are re-tested in future cycles by different team members. Write an experiment report for every test, even failed ones.
